purpose: One receipt per governed tool-call. Proves an action happened under a specific Grant, with input/output hashes and cost, so the manager projection can trace every step without reading the agent conversation.
distinct_from: artifact-receipt (per output) and evidence-receipt (per conclusion). Action Receipt is per invocation.

field_contract.work_node_id: The Work Node this action serves. Ties the receipt into the projection.
field_contract.grant_id: The active Grant that authorized this call. Verifier confirms it was not revoked/expired at call time.
field_contract.trace_id: Trace envelope shared by every action and nested callback in this Work Node.
field_contract.tool_name: The capability invoked (e.g. fff, claude_code_worker, edit_file).
field_contract.sequence: Monotonic index of this call within the node (0-based). Detects gaps/reordering.
field_contract.decision: allow | deny | freeze. deny/freeze receipts prove a governed refusal (e.g. revoked grant).
field_contract.input_hash: sha256:<64hex> of a sanitized input summary. Never the raw input; no secrets.
field_contract.output_hash: sha256:<64hex> of the output artifact, or null for deny/freeze.

cost.estimated_usd: Estimated USD cost of this call. Rolls up into cost_per_adopted_outcome.

field_contract.connector_receipt: Optional upstream receipt id/ref from the tool's own logging (MCP call id, CLI exit trace).

storage.append_only_index: The set of files under a node dir IS the receipt chain; deletion is a governance violation.

invariants: A receipt is written for every tool-call, including denied and frozen ones.
input_hash and output_hash never contain raw data; they hash a sanitized summary.
sequence must be contiguous from 0; a gap means a call was made without a receipt (verification fails).
Receipts are immutable; a correction creates a new action_id referencing the prior one via connector_receipt.
Nested callbacks must preserve trace_id, work_node_id, and Grant scope; they cannot create authority by re-entering a tool loop.
